ops.title: Window-owned scope: accepted limitations + upstream cc-interact follow-ups
ops.body:
The window-owned scope change (constant `scopeSentinel` ScopeResolve in `internal/daemon/daemon.go`) went through an adversarial review (codex finder + refuter at xhigh). Four findings were **accepted as known limitations** — all pre-existing cc-interact substrate behaviors that the global sentinel widens from same-cwd-only to any-cwd. Fixes belong upstream in cc-interact, not here:

1. **PID-recycling adoption.** `Resolver.Find`'s pid fallback (`subject/resolver.go:20`, fires only when the session lookup misses) matches on numeric `claude_pid` alone. A recycled OS pid can adopt a dead window's still-open board. Mitigated in practice because the skill always passes `--session`. Upstream fix idea: liveness-check the pid before the fallback matches, or detach subjects whose window died (BootReconcile-style).
2. **Nontransactional fresh start.** `Start(fresh=true)` runs Find → SetStatus → Detach → Create without a transaction (`subject/resolver.go:39`). Concurrent `start --new` for one window hits `UNIQUE constraint failed: subjects.session_id, subjects.scope` (~8/50 in the refuter's hammer test). Error, not corruption; retry succeeds. Upstream fix: wrap fresh-start in a transaction.
3. **`start --new` doesn't retire the old board's surface.** The resolver's fresh-close writes subject status only — no `present.closed` event — so an old browser tab still POSTs interactions successfully (REST checks event-reduced closure, `rest.go:105`) and a pinned `watch`/channel stream never ends. Upstream fix: emit a close event on fresh-start close, or filter subject-by-slug on row status.
4. **PID-less poll cross-talk.** Activity polls key on (scope, consumer, pid); two pid-less (pid 0) sessions now share (sentinel, channel, 0) and can misreport `channelState: "pending"` for ~3s. Cosmetic; only affects CLI use outside a Claude window.

Also rejected-by-design: `Start` rebinding a session's subject to a new caller pid is the intended session-rotation handoff; resume-with-`--doc` keeping interactions for matching block ids is the redraft feature (the SKILL now warns to use `--new` for unrelated boards, since a reused block id inherits stale verdicts).
